content.assessment: This is the broadest, most cross-domain proposal, requiring coordination across multiple actor types (school-choice policy, network planning, private/church sector oversight, legal compliance bodies) with no single implementing authority clearly identified. It correctly identifies a structural precondition (free-school-choice-driven segregation) into which SEN identification enters, and it proposes using a documented precedent (the 2020 readiness rule change) as a model for anticipatory impact assessment, which is a reasonable monitoring innovation. However, the proposal's own risk section acknowledges strong documented institutional resistance to structural anti-segregation reform, and no sequencing or pilot pathway is specified for overcoming this resistance before full-scale coordination is attempted.
opportunities: A narrower pilot focusing solely on extending safeguards to free school choice and selective tracks, before attempting full cross-domain coordination, could test feasibility with lower failure-containment risk
strengths: Grounds the mechanism in an established structural driver of segregation (free school choice plus residential mobility) rather than treating SEN identification as an isolated phenomenon (F-live-implementation_planning-08)
Proposes using the documented 2020 readiness rule change as a template for anticipatory cross-domain impact assessment, offering a concrete monitoring innovation grounded in precedent
threats: Documented historical precedent of institutional resistance to structural anti-segregation reform suggests a high risk of stalled or diluted implementation absent a phased, politically sequenced rollout
weaknesses: No single coordinating actor or authority is specified for the cross-domain review mechanism, leaving unclear who has the capacity or mandate to execute it

The proposal does not address how documented informal school-level gatekeeping behavior (self-limiting SNI intake) would interact with or undermine a centrally coordinated anti-segregation mechanism (F-live-implementation_planning-09)
